description_zh: McDonald's in Australia is one of the most common entry-level jobs for international students, offering systematic training and flexible scheduling. Covered by the Fast Food Industry Award, the base hourly rate for those aged 21 and over is approximately $24.10, with penalty rates for weekends and public holidays.

Australia has a strong coffee culture with high demand for baristas. You need to master making Flat Whites, Lattes, and other Australian-style coffees. Taking a short barista course ($50-100) is recommended, and experienced baristas can earn higher hourly rates.

Working in Western restaurants requires higher English proficiency, while Chinese restaurants have lower entry barriers but may also pay less. Legitimate restaurants pay according to the Restaurant Industry Award, with casual employees receiving a 25% loading. Be careful to avoid accepting cash-in-hand work below minimum wage.

Responsibilities include dishwashing, food preparation, and kitchen cleaning, with low English requirements. This is a common choice for students with limited English. The work is physically demanding and requires long periods of standing. Covered by the Hospitality Industry Award.

Deliver food through platforms like Uber Eats and DoorDash, with completely flexible hours and pay per delivery. Earnings are higher during peak hours. You need your own transport and an ABN as a sole trader. No penalty rates, but peak-time bonuses are available.

Woolworths and Coles are Australia's two major supermarkets, strictly paying under the General Retail Industry Award with transparent and guaranteed wages. Duties include checkout, restocking, and shelf organisation. Saturday rate is 1.25x, Sunday 1.5x, and public holidays 2.5x.

Work at retail stores such as Kmart, Target, or Uniqlo, covered by the General Retail Industry Award. Duties include customer service, checkout, and merchandise display. Demand is especially high during peak shopping periods like Christmas and Boxing Day.

Tutor primary and secondary school students in maths, science, or Chinese, with relatively high hourly rates. Find students through platforms like Superprof or community networks, or work as a university course tutor. Demand for Chinese language tutoring is steady, and STEM tutoring can pay $50-70 per hour.

Pick fruits and vegetables on farms, with some positions paid per piece (guaranteed no less than minimum hourly wage). Ideal for intensive work during holidays, mainly concentrated in agricultural regions of Queensland and Victoria. Physically demanding, but peak-season earnings can be substantial.

Pick, pack, and move goods in logistics warehouses, typically found through labour hire agencies such as Hays or Randstad. Night shift rates are higher (up to $30+). Physically demanding, suitable for earning intensively during holidays.

Responsible for hotel room cleaning, bed making, and restocking amenities, covered by the Hospitality Industry Award. English requirements are low, and large chain hotels offer more standardised pay. Demand is high during tourist peak seasons.

Australia has a significant ageing population, creating high demand for aged care workers. A Certificate III in Individual Support or equivalent is required, and some nursing students can work while studying. Since 2023, the aged care sector has received a 15% government-funded pay increase.

Part-time IT work such as web development, app development, and data analysis commands high hourly rates. Find work through platforms like Freelancer and Upwork, or work as a university Research Assistant. Strong programming skills are required, and the experience is valuable for post-graduation job hunting.

Work as an administrative assistant in a company or university, handling document processing, data entry, and answering phones. Higher English proficiency required, suitable for business or arts students, and helpful for gaining local Australian office experience. Find positions through university Career Centres or Seek.

Commercial cleaning (office buildings, shopping centres) or residential cleaning, covered by the Cleaning Services Award, with casual employees receiving a 25% loading. Low English requirements, relatively independent work, and some cleaning companies offer stable scheduling.

Purchase Australian health products, baby formula, etc. and ship them to China (daigou), or drive for Uber/DiDi (Australian licence required). Income is variable; both require an ABN and self-managed tax returns.
